subject: Remove irrelevant VNFSDK CSIT trigger jobs
commit_message:
Remove irrelevant VNFSDK CSIT trigger jobs

 - the two CSIT suites are triggered by merge-java jobs that do not
   produce or trigger the production of a new docker image version
   to be tested by these CSITs

Issue-ID: VNFSDK-701
Signed-off-by: Lasse Kaihlavirta <l.kaihlavirt@partner.samsung.com>
Change-Id: If1d68ebadd53e62add91391392e93757c45448ce

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Tomorrow morning I will take a look. Pls do not merge

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ci-management-jjb-verify/4986/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/ci-management-jjb-verify/4986

Patch Set 1:

If I understand correctly those two jobs will be triggered during merge-java automatically, so they do not need to contain that job as "trigger_job", however if we remove them and that section is empty, do we still need to have:
"
functionality:
      - 'testsuites':
          trigger_jobs:  
"
since they are empty ?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> If I understand correctly those two jobs will be triggered during merge-java automatically, so they do not need to contain that job as "trigger_job", however if we remove them and that section is empty, do we still need to have:
> "
> functionality:
>       - 'testsuites':
>           trigger_jobs:  
> "
> since they are empty ?

I'm not sure what you mean by the first part? The trigger_job definition is specifically what triggers these two CSIT jobs (there is no other triggering mechanism outside of this), and these should be removed because CSITs rely on *docker images* and these merge-java jobs do not produce any new version of the docker images - in other words, there is nothing new to test for the CSITs even after the merge-java has executed successfully.
Ideally, these trigger jobs should be replaced with something that *does* produce the docker images that CSITs are testing (in this case vnfsdk-refrepo-maven-docker-stage-master), but I would propose against that for now since
1) I am working on improving the verification chain for Honolulu under https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Moving+CSIT+to+project+repositories and this trigger should probably change again as part of that
2) The CSITs are already being scheduled to run daily anyway at the moment, and adding trigger from vnfsdk-refrepo-maven-docker-stage-master (which also runs every day) would double the executions for these CSIT for no obvious additional benefit

BTW, now that I have your attention... one of your tests is using nexus3.onap.org:10001/onap/vnfsdk/refrepo/postgres:latest, but this image hasn't been produced for over a year - what's up with that?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> If I understand correctly those two jobs will be triggered during merge-java automatically, so they do not need to contain that job as "trigger_job", however if we remove them and that section is empty, do we still need to have:
> "
> functionality:
>       - 'testsuites':
>           trigger_jobs:  
> "
> since they are empty ?

Forgot to commen the second part of your question... I haven't seen any jjb definition completely without trigger_jobs so I have been implicitly assuming that it is required regardless of the value (or lack thereof)

> Ideally, these trigger jobs should be replaced with something that *does* produce the docker images that CSITs are testing (in this case vnfsdk-refrepo-maven-docker-stage-master), but I would propose against that for now since
> 1) I am working on improving the verification chain for Honolulu under https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Moving+CSIT+to+project+repositories and this trigger should probably change again as part of that
> 2) The CSITs are already being scheduled to run daily anyway at the moment, and adding trigger from vnfsdk-refrepo-maven-docker-stage-master (which also runs every day) would double the executions for these CSIT for no obvious additional benefit
> 
> BTW, now that I have your attention... one of your tests is using nexus3.onap.org:10001/onap/vnfsdk/refrepo/postgres:latest, but this image hasn't been produced for over a year - what's up with that?

Yes, you have right and triggering CSITs at merge can be removed. Current job configuration is bad.

but

I would like to have an option to run CSITs after executing vnfsdk-refrepo-maven-docker-stage-master job. We very often run this job manually to create an image and release it, but before that we need to run CSITs to confirm that produced image contains working application. Or is it possible to run CSITs using some magic word, for example: run-csit?

According to postgres image:
These CSIT tests were turned off from one year. Two-three weeks ago we have activated these CSITs. This postgres:latest image was created in the older version of vnfsdk, as I know. Current implementation uses a default postgres image. I will update csits and remove this strange/old image.

Patch Set 1:

> I would like to have an option to run CSITs after executing vnfsdk-refrepo-maven-docker-stage-master job. We very often run this job manually to create an image and release it, but before that we need to run CSITs to confirm that produced image contains working application. Or is it possible to run CSITs using some magic word, for example: run-csit?

Magic word given from where exactly? Are you talking about review comments in gerrit (in the same vein as "recheck" retriggers current jobs)? The problem there is that CSITs are not currently linked to gerrit reviews in any way (which is precisely what I'm aiming to change in Honolulu).

Of course, you can - and ideally you should - always run the CSITs *locally* with locally built docker images before you even push the change to review. You can therefore obviously also run the CSIT locally with new released image once that is available in csit repo in your case with commands

./run-csit.sh plans/vnfsdk-marketplace/testsuites/

./run-csit.sh plans/vnfsdk-refrepo/testsuites

I just tested those myself and they seem to be working without any extra trouble (you might have to take some manual steps to ensure that you really get the wanted image from upstream Nexus though)

> Current implementation uses a default postgres image. I will update csits and remove this strange/old image.

Excellent, that's one trouble off my list then

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > I would like to have an option to run CSITs after executing vnfsdk-refrepo-maven-docker-stage-master job. We very often run this job manually to create an image and release it, but before that we need to run CSITs to confirm that produced image contains working application. Or is it possible to run CSITs using some magic word, for example: run-csit?
> 
> Magic word given from where exactly? Are you talking about review comments in gerrit (in the same vein as "recheck" retriggers current jobs)? The problem there is that CSITs are not currently linked to gerrit reviews in any way (which is precisely what I'm aiming to change in Honolulu).
> 
> Of course, you can - and ideally you should - always run the CSITs *locally* with locally built docker images before you even push the change to review. You can therefore obviously also run the CSIT locally with new released image once that is available in csit repo in your case with commands
>

> 
> > Current implementation uses a default postgres image. I will update csits and remove this strange/old image.
> 
> Excellent, that's one trouble off my list then

> Magic word given from where exactly?
From gerrit comments. Ok, I understand. It is not possible to run csits using magic words, but why it is not possible to rerun CI job from Jenkins (no rerun button in Jenkins menu for dedicated build)? I know that Jenkins has such option, but in ONAP Jenkins this option is turned off. Why?

> Of course, you can - and ideally you should - always run the CSITs *locally* with locally built
But I don't want to run CSITs manually :( I would like to have automatic CSITs start after an image creation - ideal solution for me.

I will mark this MR as +1, because current Csit Jobs CI are badly implemented, but for me it will be better to have trigger action after vnfsdk-refrepo-maven-docker-stage-master build.

Patch Set 1:

> why it is not possible to rerun CI job from Jenkins (no rerun button in Jenkins menu for dedicated build)? I know that Jenkins has such option, but in ONAP Jenkins this option is turned off. Why?

This is an excellent question that I've wondered myself too. I assume this has been disabled by LF to reduce the load on Jenkins, but I think this is a bit misguided way of doing it (especially when we are running dozens of scheduled CSIT jobs every day that are testing obsolete images)
 
> But I don't want to run CSITs manually :( 

You really, really should (regardless of what's provided by Jenkins). This will be one of my main themes in Honolulu, because it becomes much more important if/when CSITs actually can start blocking merges. 

> I will mark this MR as +1, because current Csit Jobs CI are badly implemented, but for me it will be better to have trigger action after vnfsdk-refrepo-maven-docker-stage-master build.

Note though that you seem to have survived quite well without it until now :)

Patch Set 1:

> > do we still need to have:
> > "
> > functionality:
> >       - 'testsuites':
> >           trigger_jobs:  
> > "
> > since they are empty ?
> 
> Forgot to commen the second part of your question... I haven't seen any jjb definition completely without trigger_jobs so I have been implicitly assuming that it is required regardless of the value (or lack thereof)

FYI, just tested this in sandbox and trigger_jobs is indeed mandatory even if it has no value.
Change has been successfully merged by Jessica Wagantall
